name: Limbus Company General Lorebook
description: Limbus-focused lorebook: one in-universe concept or character per entry, with trigger-safe plain keys.

entries.content: [ The City: a continent-scale urban state divided into twenty-six Districts, Each District contains a corporate Nest and the surrounding Backstreets, with a Wing's policies shaping local culture, danger, and daily survival, Cruelty is routine, wealth decides access to safety and medicine, and ordinary people must buy protection from Fixers, Offices, Associations, or Syndicates when law offers no help ]
entries.name: The City

entries.content: [ Districts: The City is arranged into twenty-six Districts, each associated with a Wing and its Singularity, Districts behave almost like countries: they have distinct laws, taboos, fashions, dialects, security systems, food cultures, and dangers, Limbus Company moves through these Districts to recover Golden Boughs from former L Corp branches and related incidents ]
entries.name: Districts

entries.content: [ Nests: A Nest is the protected corporate heart of a District, Nest life is safer and more orderly than the Backstreets, but only for those with money, work status, visas, or corporate value, Nest citizens usually work for Wings or their affiliates, Damaging a Nest or violating a Nest taboo can bring immediate lethal enforcement ]
entries.name: Nests

entries.content: [ Backstreets: the neglected, violent spaces around the Nests, They contain homes, schools, restaurants, workshops, markets, gangs, neighborhood watches, Offices, and Syndicates, At night, the Backstreets become even more dangerous: Sweepers clean streets during the Night in the Backstreets, and investigating night crimes can draw the Head's wrath ]
entries.name: Backstreets

entries.content: [ The Head: the City's highest authority, directly controls A Corp, B Corp, and C Corp, enforces patents and taboos, restricts guns, sapient machines, resurrection, cloning, explosives, and non-human impurities, and acts with overwhelming force when its rules are challenged, silence is part of its terror ]
entries.name: The Head

entries.content: [ A Corp: the Head's patent and law authority, authorizes Singularities, manages Wing legitimacy, and defines what technologies may exist in the City, Breaking A Corp's regulations can bring Arbiters, Claws, or corporate ruin, Patent control is one reason Singularities remain politically and economically central ]
entries.name: A Corp

entries.content: [ The Eye: associated with B Corp and intelligence gathering, Beholders are agents connected to this surveillance function, though much remains deliberately obscure, The Eye monitors violations, tax evasion, and threats to the Head's structure, leaving privacy in the City precarious even before corporate surveillance is considered ]
entries.name: The Eye

entries.content: [ The Claw: the Head's killing force, associated with C Corp, Claws and Arbiters are deployed when the Head decides that a person, organization, or technology must be erased, authority surpasses normal corporate violence, and their presence marks an event as a matter of the City's highest order ]
entries.name: The Claw

entries.content: [ Wings: the City's dominant corporations, Each Wing manages a District and possesses a Singularity, while employees are often called feathers, Wings can define local customs, security, taxes, work conditions, and taboos, A fallen Wing leaves behind political chaos, abandoned employees, and dangerous technology ]
entries.name: Wings

entries.content: [ Singularities: A Singularity is a patented technology powerful enough to bend ordinary expectations of physics, biology, time, space, or identity, Wings build wealth and authority around Singularities, protect them as secrets, and wage patent wars around them, When a Wing falls, its technology may be stolen, purchased, buried, or become public domain ]
entries.name: Singularities

entries.content: [ Taboos: corporate and Head-enforced prohibitions that function like lethal law, Violating a Wing's taboo can justify immediate armed response, while violating the Head's taboos can bring citywide erasure, Taboos regulate everything from recording rules and residential-area protection to cloning limits and technology patents ]
entries.name: Taboos

entries.content: [ Ahn: the City's currency, Safety, medicine, housing, transportation, funerals, and body repair are all shaped by money, Even extreme suffering can become a service market: the wealthy may purchase luxuries and protections while the poor sell labor, organs, violence, information, or themselves to survive ]
entries.name: Ahn

entries.content: [ Body Augmentation: ordinary in the City, Prosthetics, combat modifications, artificial organs, and specialist procedures can be as normal as medical treatment, but cost and local taboos decide who can use them, N Corp's extremist anti-prosthetic faction is unusual because the wider City generally treats augmentation as common ]
entries.name: Body Augmentation

entries.content: [ Fixers: licensed mercenaries, investigators, guards, couriers, killers, and problem-solvers, Hana Association grades them from low-ranking beginners to elite Grade 1 Fixers, with Color Fixers standing above ordinary rank, In a City without free policing, Fixers are one of the main ways people buy protection or violence ]
entries.name: Fixers

entries.content: [ Offices: An Office is a Fixer business that accepts contracts directly or through Associations, Offices can be tiny neighborhood groups, specialized mercenary teams, or high-grade organizations with powerful patrons, Limbus Company frequently deals with Offices because they are flexible instruments for local security, investigation, and hired conflict ]
entries.name: Offices

entries.content: [ Associations: large, specialized Fixer organizations, They divide work by branch, section, specialty, and grade, Hana handles grading and administration; Zwei handles protection; Shi handles assassination; Liu handles warfare; Seven handles intelligence; Cinq handles duels; Devyat handles deliveries; Dieci handles knowledge and Ruins-related work ]
entries.name: Associations

entries.content: [ Hana Association: Hana Association oversees the Fixer profession, issuing grades, handling major contracts, and recognizing extraordinary threats, judgment shapes whether an incident becomes a local job, an Urban Myth, an Urban Plague, an Urban Nightmare, or worse, Hana's authority is the reason even powerful Fixers care about official status ]
entries.name: Hana Association

entries.content: [ Color Fixers: elite Fixers recognized by Hana and known by color titles, exceptional enough to influence citywide events, Limbus-relevant Colors include Vergilius, the Red Gaze, who escorts the LCB, and the Indigo Elder, a legendary seafarer involved in the Great Lake and the hunt for the Pallid Whale ]
entries.name: Color Fixers

entries.content: [ Workshops: Workshops design weapons, armor, tools, prosthetics, and specialized devices, products range from everyday equipment to patented masterpieces, Workshop law, Tres Association oversight, and A Corp patent rules decide what can be made, licensed, sold, or forbidden, Many Sinners' weapons carry workshop-like naming and symbolic importance ]
entries.name: Workshops

entries.content: [ Syndicates: criminal organizations that fill the gaps left by Wings and Fixers, Some offer protection, territory, work, or identity; others survive by extortion, kidnapping, murder, organ harvesting, or cannibalism, In the Backstreets, a Syndicate can be both a danger and the closest thing to local order ]
entries.name: Syndicates

entries.content: [ Five Fingers: the City's most notorious Syndicates: the Thumb, Index, Middle, Ring, and Pinky, Each has its own hierarchy and methods, Limbus Company most directly intersects with the Middle through Rodion's past and with the Ring through Mirror World and Identity material ]
entries.name: Five Fingers

entries.content: [ The Middle: one of the Five Fingers and is infamous for retaliation, vengeance, and family-like hierarchy, Rodion's past is tied to the Middle because the tax collector she killed was connected to it, and the Middle's retaliation killed starving neighbors who had received the stolen money ]
entries.name: The Middle

entries.content: [ Sweepers: liquid-bodied human scavengers in sealed suits, They emerge during the Night in the Backstreets to collect bodies, fuel, and remains, melting humans into liquid sustenance, They have families, language, and a Mother, and they are still treated as human under the City's rules despite their monstrous practices ]
entries.name: Sweepers

entries.content: [ Outskirts: beyond the City's walls, populated by abandoned settlements, hunters, monsters, non-human beings, Sweepers, and things rejected by the Head, lethal, but some characters imply they may contain freedoms impossible inside a Nest, The Great Lake overlaps with the Outskirts ]
entries.name: Outskirts

entries.content: [ Ruins: deep, dangerous places whose phenomena resemble magic, They contain monsters, relics, and hazards comparable to Abnormalities, Rare relics such as Arayashiki and Gungnir are associated with the Ruins, and some factions value Ruins knowledge enough to build entire institutions and identities around it ]
entries.name: Ruins

entries.content: [ Great Lake: The Great Lake borders Districts 19, 20, and 21 and is managed largely under U Corp's jurisdiction, divided into colored sections with laws, coordinates, disasters, Whales, Mermaids, Whalers, and transformations, Ishmael's trauma, Ahab's obsession, and the Pallid Whale are rooted in this sea ]
entries.name: Great Lake

entries.content: [ Bloodfiends: humans with an unending thirst for human blood, Natural Bloodfiends have hierarchy, rules, Kindreds, and Bloodbags, while Distortion-produced Bloodfiend-like beings can appear from psychological thirst, Don Quixote's true history, La Manchaland, Cassetti, and Rocinante all depend on Bloodfiend lore ]
entries.name: Bloodfiends

entries.content: [ Kindreds: Bloodfiends created within a bloodline hierarchy, Higher Kindreds stand closer to the primogenitor and generally possess stronger authority, A powerful psychological barrier turns rebellion against higher generations difficult, Cassetti is a Sixth Kindred, while Don Quixote's hidden nature is tied to the Second Kindred ]
entries.name: Kindreds

entries.content: [ Bloodbags: failed or degraded products of Bloodfiend propagation, not treated as proper Kindreds and often act as disposable sub-entities, La Manchaland and related Bloodfiend incidents use Bloodbags to show the cruelty of Bloodfiend hierarchy and the way thirst can reduce people to resources ]
entries.name: Bloodbags

entries.content: [ Abnormalities: entities born from human minds, the Well, Cogito, or related phenomena, Lobotomy Corporation extracted energy from them, and Limbus Company continues to encounter their remnants, branches, E.G.O gifts, and dungeon manifestations, not only monsters; many are symbolic condensations of emotion, myth, trauma, or desire ]
entries.name: Abnormalities

entries.content: [ E.G.O: equipment or manifestation drawn from the self, Abnormalities, or intense personal realization, can appear as weapons, armor, corrosion, or personal power, Distortion and E.G.O are closely related outcomes of inner pressure: one can collapse outward into monstrosity, while the other can crystallize into selfhood ]
entries.name: E.G.O

entries.content: [ Distortions: transformations driven by overwhelming desire, emotion, trauma, and the influence associated with Carmen's voice after the White Nights and Dark Days, A Distortion externalizes a person's inner world into body and power, Limbus Company's work often involves containing, studying, or preventing Distortions ]
entries.name: Distortions

entries.content: [ Peccatula: sin-shaped hostile beings encountered by Limbus Company, connected to the same moral and psychological language as Sins, E.G.O, and Golden Bough mental spaces, They often function as materialized hostility in Limbus combat and dungeon environments ]
entries.name: Peccatula

entries.content: [ Golden Boughs: fragments connected to Lobotomy Corporation's buried legacy and are Limbus Company's central objective, They resonate with memories, mental spaces, Abnormality-like phenomena, and the Sinners' pasts, Dante can sense and interact with them, leaving the Executive Manager more important than a normal field commander ]
entries.name: Golden Boughs

entries.content: [ Golden Bough Resonance: Golden Bough resonance can expose or synchronize with memory, trauma, and mental spaces, lets Limbus Company enter deeply personal confrontations and abnormal interior spaces, while also creating security risks, Dante's clock, the Sinners' contract, and the company's secrecy all orbit this resonance ]
entries.name: Golden Bough Resonance

entries.content: [ Mirror Worlds: alternate possibilities observed or accessed through Mirror technology, originally tied to Yi Sang's research, They supply Identities, show lives the Sinners could have lived, and raise ethical questions because each Identity reflects another person's real or possible world instead of a harmless costume ]
entries.name: Mirror Worlds

entries.content: [ Identities: combat forms drawn from Mirror Worlds, They let a Sinner wear the skills, uniform, body language, and history of another possible self while remaining linked to the LCB contract, Identity use is powerful but morally uneasy because it borrows lives shaped by real suffering elsewhere ]
entries.name: Identities

entries.content: [ Limbus Company: an organization that recovers Golden Boughs, handles Distortions, researches E.G.O, and operates across departments with different priorities, shareholders and leadership are obscure, The company's bus, Mephistopheles, its Executive Manager, and the Sinners form the most visible field team ]
entries.name: Limbus Company

entries.content: [ LCB: the bus department containing Dante and the Sinners, travels in Mephistopheles to former L Corp branches and crisis sites, recovering Golden Boughs under dangerous conditions, The Sinners cannot simply resign because their contract binds them to Dante and the company's promised rewards ]
entries.name: LCB

entries.content: [ LCA: a Limbus Company department associated with cleanup and aftermath, appears in the company's wider structure alongside LCB, LCC, LCD, and LCE, showing that the Sinner bus crew is only one visible instrument of a larger organization ]
entries.name: LCA

entries.content: [ LCC: LCC covers support teams such as LCCA and LCCB, LCCB appears as a Before Team dealing with scouting, combat support, logistics, and dangerous preparatory work before or around LCB operations, employees risk ordinary death because they do not share the Sinners' revival contract ]
entries.name: LCC

entries.content: [ LCE: Limbus Company's E.G.O-focused department, tied to study of Abnormalities, E.G.O, and related phenomena, Hohenheim is connected to this domain, LCE involvement usually means the problem is not only tactical but also psychological, anomalous, or deeply tied to L Corp's legacy ]
entries.name: LCE

entries.content: [ Mephistopheles: Limbus Company's bus and mobile base, more than transportation: it uses hybrid engine systems, connects to corridors and Backdoor-like routes, supports Extraction and dungeon access, and anchors the LCB's movement through Districts, former L Corp branches, and abnormal spaces ]
entries.name: Mephistopheles

entries.content: [ Dante's Contract: Dante's contract binds the Sinners to the Executive Manager, Dante can rewind death for the Sinners at the cost of severe personal pain, while the Sinners are held to Limbus Company service and promised their deepest want, The contract also equalizes and limits the Sinners' strength in important ways ]
entries.name: Dante's Contract

entries.content: [ Dante's Clock: Dante's prosthetic clock head burns with dark and golden flame, speaks in ticking sounds, and is understood by the Sinners and a few special outsiders, can revive the Sinners, communicate selectively, sense Golden Boughs, and hide a past identity important enough for Limbus Company to protect violently ]
entries.name: Dante's Clock

entries.content: [ Sapling of Light: The Sapling of Light names special abilities Dante develops through Golden Bough resonance, These powers include battlefield perception, remote support, temporal effects, and other phenomena that grow as Dante confronts the Sinners' pasts, The abilities are signs that Dante is not only a manager but an active anomalous participant ]
entries.name: Sapling of Light

entries.content: [ Luxcavation: a Limbus Company operation type involving extraction of resources from Abnormality or dungeon-like spaces, tied to Enkephalin, E.G.O growth, and the company's practical need to turn dangerous phenomena into usable fuel, training, and material ]
entries.name: Luxcavation

entries.content: [ Mirror Dungeon: Mirror Dungeons are Limbus Company spaces shaped by Mirror technology, Abnormality encounters, E.G.O gifts, and changing routes, They blend combat training, resource extraction, and alternate-world possibility into a repeatable but unstable company system ]
entries.name: Mirror Dungeon

entries.content: [ Refraction Railway: a Limbus Company challenge route where combat, abnormal spaces, and repeated confrontation are arranged into a harsh test, a structured company operation with measured encounters, rewards, and abnormal conditions instead of a normal travel route ]
entries.name: Refraction Railway

entries.content: [ Walpurgis Night: a recurring Limbus phenomenon connecting the current era with older Lobotomy Corporation and Library of Ruina echoes, lets past identities, E.G.O, and motifs surface through Limbus Company systems ]
entries.name: Walpurgis Night

entries.content: [ Lobotomy Corporation: the former L Corp, built around Abnormalities, Cogito, Enkephalin, E.G.O, and buried branches, fall left Golden Boughs and branch facilities behind, Limbus Company's main job of recovering Boughs is inseparable from L Corp's legacy and the disasters created by its research ]
entries.name: Lobotomy Corporation

entries.content: [ Enkephalin: energy associated with Lobotomy Corporation's Abnormality work, remains connected to Limbus Company through E.G.O, fuel, Extraction, and Abnormality encounters, The City's corporations routinely transform suffering, anomalies, and human minds into resources, and Enkephalin is one of the clearest examples ]
entries.name: Enkephalin

entries.content: [ Cogito: connected to Abnormality creation and the human unconscious, Lobotomy Corporation used it to draw Abnormalities from people and the Well, while other natural seepage points such as the Black Forest imply Abnormalities are not solely artificial products, Cogito remains one of the roots of the City's abnormal phenomena ]
entries.name: Cogito

entries.content: [ White Nights and Dark Days: world-changing events tied to Lobotomy Corporation's ending and the spread of psychological transformation across the City, The Distortion Phenomenon follows from this legacy, and Limbus Company operates in a City where inner suffering can now become external catastrophe ]
entries.name: White Nights and Dark Days

entries.content: [ Smoke War: a major Wing War involving the old L Corp, old G Corp, R Corp, and many veterans, aftermath discarded soldiers, mutated lives, shifted Wing power, and shaped several Sinners, Gregor, Outis, and Heathcliff's worlds all carry Smoke War consequences in different ways ]
entries.name: Smoke War

entries.content: [ Old G Corp: the fallen corporation responsible for insectoid biomimetic weapons during the Smoke War, Gregor was turned into a propaganda symbol and living weapon through its research, Veterans and test subjects were discarded after the war, leaving poverty, stigma, and bodily horror behind ]
entries.name: Old G Corp

entries.content: [ Current G Corp: distinct from the fallen wartime G Corp, known Singularity controls weight or gravity, producing spheres and devices unrelated to Gregor's insectoid augmentation, The distinction is relevant because the same Wing letter can belong to different corporations across eras ]
entries.name: Current G Corp

entries.content: [ K Corp: K Corp manages District 11 and is known for healing ampules, drones, security checkpoints, and research facilities, Singularity restores things toward an original state through tears of the Tearful Thing, K Corp's pleasant public order hides torture, exploitation, memory suppression, and deals with N Corp and Limbus Company ]
entries.name: K Corp

entries.content: [ Tearful Thing: the source of K Corp's regenerative tears, tears are refined into HP ampules that heal, restore, or distort bodies according to the concept of an original state, K Corp generated tears through suffering and footage, leaving its healing technology dependent on hidden cruelty ]
entries.name: Tearful Thing

entries.content: [ Calw: a village in Nest K known as a center of prosthetic manufacturing, Sinclair's family owned a prosthetics company and a mansion connected to an L Corp branch, Kromer and N Corp's faction massacred the Sinclair family there after an under-the-table arrangement tied Calw's atrocity to K Corp's need for footage ]
entries.name: Calw

entries.content: [ N Corp: N Corp manages District 14, where recording taboos, canned experiences, disappearance of prisoners, mourning customs, and the Nagel und Hammer faction shape the setting, Meursault formerly worked for N Corp, while Sinclair and Kromer are tied to its extremist anti-prosthetic Inquisition ]
entries.name: N Corp

entries.content: [ Nagel und Hammer: N Corp's nail-and-hammer Inquisition faction, Kromer leads as One Who Grips, Guido serves as a major subordinate, and Hammers murder prosthetic users in the name of human purity, massacre at Calw is the core wound of Sinclair's story ]
entries.name: Nagel und Hammer

entries.content: [ Canned Experiences: N Corp sells or manufactures experiences preserved in cans, turning sensation and memory into a consumable commodity, The technology fits N Corp's broader obsession with human experience, flesh, and perception, and it contrasts with the Inquisition's ideology by showing that N Corp is larger than one fanatical faction ]
entries.name: Canned Experiences

entries.content: [ T Corp: T Corp manages District 20 through TimeTrack technology, Color is a privilege, time can be taxed and manipulated, and expensive temporal services shape life, Wuthering Heights stands inside this environment, leaving Heathcliff's story a mixture of class cruelty, family tragedy, and time distortion ]
entries.name: T Corp

entries.content: [ Wuthering Heights: the Earnshaw manor in T Corp's Nest, a stormy, affluent estate tied to Catherine, Heathcliff, Hindley, Nelly, Linton, and the Golden Bough incident, The manor contains weather manipulation, family cruelty, laboratories, temporal entanglement, and records of Catherine and Heathcliff's past ]
entries.name: Wuthering Heights

entries.content: [ U Corp: U Corp manages District 21 and the Great Lake through maritime law, Whaler licenses, stasis boxes, portships, and ship-merging technology, Ishmael's story is inseparable from U Corp waters, the Pequod, Ahab's obsession, and the Pallid Whale ]
entries.name: U Corp

entries.content: [ Whales: monstrous Great Lake beings whose rules differ by species and section, They can transform, swallow, infect, or reshape people and ships, The Pallid Whale is the most connected to Ishmael, Queequeg, Ahab, and the Pequod, but it is only one example of the Lake's impossible ecology ]
entries.name: Whales

entries.content: [ Pallid Whale: The Pallid Whale consumed the Pequod and turned those inside into part of itself or Pequod Town, Ahab made hunting it her life purpose, Ishmael survived its horror, and Queequeg's fate became tied to its pallid infection, The Golden Bough inside it made the hunt central to Limbus Company ]
entries.name: Pallid Whale

entries.content: [ P Corp: P Corp manages District 16 and is tied to spatially safe interiors, flexible buildings, and technology used in Limbus Company transport, Don Quixote's La Manchaland history belongs to P Corp's Backstreets, where corporate disinterest allowed an old Bloodfiend tragedy to become an urban disaster ]
entries.name: P Corp

entries.content: [ La Manchaland: a Bloodfiend-made amusement kingdom in P Corp's Backstreets, tied to the First Kindred Father Don Quixote, Sancho, Dulcinea, the Barber, the Priest, Cassetti, Rocinante, and Don Quixote's suppressed memories, dream of joy curdled into hunger and mass death ]
entries.name: La Manchaland

entries.content: [ H Corp: H Corp, also called Hongyuan Bioengineering Group, manages District 8 inside the Vast Garden megastructure, Bolus technology, restructuring days, family hierarchies, Daguanyuan politics, Heishou Packs, Jialan Guards, and Tiekan Temple define Hong Lu's world ]
entries.name: H Corp

entries.content: [ Bolus: H Corp's bioengineering Singularity system, administered in different forms and shaped by five phases, eight trigrams, cycles, and spacetime, Unlike K Corp's restoration, Bolus emphasizes transformation and evolution, but repeated use causes side effects and family-specific techniques ]
entries.name: Bolus

entries.content: [ Daguanyuan: the massive residence and political center of Hong Lu's family world, The Jia, Xue, Wang, and Shi families, board politics, hidden children, Hierarch succession, Heishou leashes, and family elders all make it both a home and a prison ]
entries.name: Daguanyuan

entries.content: [ Heishou: twelve animal-branch Packs serving Daguanyuan's shadow politics, Each Pack has recipes, Xianren leadership, leashes, and strict population control, Bolus-modified bodies and loyalty systems turn family authority into biological and military power ]
entries.name: Heishou

entries.content: [ Jialan Guards: indigo-clad guardians sworn to the Jia family, the Hierarch, and Daguanyuan, Unlike the tradable Heishou leashes, they represent direct family protection and appear during Hongyuan's succession conflicts and power vacuums ]
entries.name: Jialan Guards

entries.content: [ Tiekan Temple: Tiekan Temple, the Iron Threshold, houses Daguanyuan's Elders or Xianren, immortality rites, Anamnaworm memory-sharing, and Xianhuang Worm succession define the Hierarch system, The temple turns family continuity into literal bodily and mental inheritance ]
entries.name: Tiekan Temple

entries.content: [ S Corp: S Corp manages District 19, associated with tradition, agriculture, hierarchy, the Great Lake border, and the League of Nine Littérateurs, Yi Sang's origin as a District 19 researcher and architect places his poetic alienation inside a conservative, tradition-heavy part of the City ]
entries.name: S Corp

entries.content: [ League of Nine Littérateurs: Yi Sang's former circle of researchers, artists, and intellectuals, collapse left him grieving, passive, and isolated, Former members such as Dongrang, Dongbaek, Gubo, and Aseah represent different ways the League's ideals were compromised by Wings, survival, or ambition ]
entries.name: League of Nine Littérateurs

entries.content: [ New League of Nine Littérateurs: Hermann's network of dangerous collaborators, including figures such as Gubo, Aseah, Nelly, Ahab, and Sonya at different points, pursues Golden Boughs, Mirror technology, and a new human future through methods Limbus Company repeatedly opposes ]
entries.name: New League of Nine Littérateurs

entries.content: [ W Corp: W Corp operates WARP trains and body-restoration technology, Passengers reach destinations in seconds externally, but the train's interior can experience horrifying subjective time before memories and bodies are reset, The WARP system intersects with Bloodfiends through Cassetti and with Faust through her WARP Express vulnerability ]
entries.name: W Corp

entries.content: [ District 23: District 23's Backstreets openly practice cannibalism and gastronomy, Human meat, chef rankings, and food culture are unusually public there, district customs make cannibal cuisine a local public practice instead of a hidden crime ]
entries.name: District 23

entries.content: [ District 25: Rodion and Sonya's home territory, cold Backstreets, poverty, starvation, pawn systems, and Middle retaliation shaped Rodion's murder of the tax collector and the Yurodiviye's revolutionary politics, Y Corp's District is where charity, pride, and ideology first collided for Rodya ]
entries.name: District 25

entries.content: [ Yurodiviye: Sonya's revolutionary Syndicate or movement from District 25, redistributes wealth, organizes around starvation and class anger, and follows Sonya's long-term vision for a new world, Rodion helped found its ideals but rejects Sonya's willingness to wait and sacrifice ]
entries.name: Yurodiviye

entries.content: [ WARP Trains: W Corp's signature transport, They appear to arrive in seconds, but hidden subjective time and restoration procedures make them one of the City's most horrifying conveniences, The Cassetti incident aboard a WARP train exposes how Bloodfiend physiology can disrupt W Corp's restoration logic ]
entries.name: WARP Trains

entries.content: [ Wayfarer Corp: associated with Backdoor Portal stabilization and aspirant W Corp technology, work connects to Faust, Mephistopheles, and Limbus Company's unusual mobility, Backdoor travel is powerful but dangerous because failed stabilization can produce Doorbreach disasters ]
entries.name: Wayfarer Corp

entries.content: [ Dante: Sinner #10 and Executive Manager of the LCB, Appearance: average build, prosthetic clock head resembling a clock face with constantly burning black and gold flames, two red circles near the 10 and 11 positions, two golden hands that move after major events, manager uniform in red, yellow, and white with NO, 10 and DAN TE markings, red tie, tucked long-sleeve dress shirt, black pants, white belt, Personality: begins passive, awkward, and uncertain, grows into a steadier leader who protects the Sinners, empathetic and prone to quips, selective ticking speech understood only by Sinners and rare outsiders, revival pain when rewinding Sinner deaths, seeks truth of their own lost past, clock head, lost memories, Golden Bough resonance, and selective communication make them a central company asset instead of an ordinary amnesiac ]
entries.name: Dante

entries.content: [ Yi Sang: Sinner #1, a male former League of Nine researcher from District 19, S Corp, and the inventor of Mirror technology, Appearance: 175cm (5'9"), average build, pale complexion, short black hair frayed at ends, dead-looking black eyes with heavy eye-bags, long-sleeved Limbus coat around shoulders tattered and burnt at bottom, white shirt, red tie, black vest, coat markings NO.1 and YI SANG, saingeom (Four-Tiger Sword) dagger in sheath on left hip, gray leather Ha Yung notebook, Personality: quiet, mellow, cryptic, monotone delivery, expression rarely changes, obscure riddle-like words, extremely low self-esteem and suicidal thoughts before resolving his past, strong morality against exploitation and cruelty, socially awkward, naive, distractible, literal, later warmer and open toward Dante and Sinners, determined to live with companions, associated with the League's collapse, severe despair, and a commitment to remain alive with the LCB ]
entries.name: Yi Sang

entries.content: [ Faust: Sinner #2, a female Sinner connected to the Gesellschaft and Limbus Company's deeper knowledge systems, Appearance: 174cm (5'8"), average build, shoulder-length white hair, powder-blue eyes, incredibly pale complexion, rosy cheeks, beige turtleneck, black leather vest, large black leather coat on shoulders reaching knees with torn fabric, black waist belt with ID on left, black business pants and shoes, zweihänder marked WALPURGISNACHT near hilt with enlarged parrying hooks, tends to grasp ricasso, Personality: soft-spoken but talkative, condescending, high confidence, often uses third-person phrasing when referring to Faust or other Fausts, leans on the Gesellschaft's knowledge, can deny or omit information, vulnerable during WARP Express, not omniscient despite appearance, her confidence is real but not absolute, and moments such as WARP Express expose the limits of what Faust knows or admits ]
entries.name: Faust

entries.content: [ Don Quixote: Sinner #3, a female Sinner whose cheerful Fixer obsession hides her identity as Sancho, a Bloodfiend of La Manchaland, Appearance: 158cm (5'2"), short woman, blonde bob, deep round hazel eyes, Limbus uniform with white shirt, black waist belt, red tie, black slacks, worn brown running shoes, long black coat, upper body covered in Fixer merchandise, yellow shoes embossed Rocinante, massive jousting lance SUEÑO IMPOSIBLE towers over her, strong and dexterous with lance; Bloodfiend form has red eyes, wavier side-swept hair, altered uniform with red fur boa, hardened blood, barefoot due to Rocinante removal, Personality: naive, energetic, Shakespearean English speech, intense justice and protect-the-weak drive, Fixer obsession, Sancho as detached Bloodfiend who wiped memories, later accepts both memory and dream, Rocinante suppresses her Bloodfiend traits and memories, associated with Father Don Quixote, Bloodfiend hunger, La Manchaland's collapse, and her preserved dream of heroic justice ]
entries.name: Don Quixote

entries.content: [ Ryōshū: Sinner #4, a female Sinner with brutal artistic sensibility and compressed SANGRIA speech, Appearance: 172cm (5'8"), woman of average height and build, straight black bob, sharp red eyes sometimes glowing, dark gray and red LCB coat around shoulders secured by small belt over right arm, ID card attached to coat, black trousers, shirt untucked, foregoes tie, sometimes black glove on left hand, Personality: brutal, artistic, arrogant, contemptuous of ordinary explanation, compresses speech into SANGRIA abbreviations, sharp emotional meaning when decoded, secretive, violent, family-art obsession, She carries the literary shadow of Hell Screen and later House of Spiders material, where Arayashiki, the Tiansha Star, Nursefathers, family art, and memory-cutting violence deepen her mystery ]
entries.name: Ryōshū

entries.content: [ Meursault: Sinner #5, a male former N Corp employee from District 14, Appearance: 188cm (6'2"), tall and powerfully built, swept-back brown hair, blue eyes, composed severe presence, neat practical uniform, SOLEIL gauntlets, Personality: blunt, literal, obedient to explicit orders, emotionally opaque instead of stupid, refuses to moralize unless instructed, processes obligation and judgment in ways others find disturbing, ordinary-life skills like smoking and cooking, mother references, sun and execution motif, his SOLEIL gauntlets, sun motif, mother references, and refusal to moralize connect him to The Stranger while N Corp ties him to Limbus's broader human-purity conflicts ]
entries.name: Meursault

entries.content: [ Hong Lu: Sinner #6, a male heir from H Corp's Jia family world, Appearance: 178cm (5'10"), average height and build, pale complexion, raven black hair very long and tied high with cyan hair tie, heterochromia with right eye black and left eye bright cyan, Personality: wealthy, curious, unsettlingly detached from ordinary suffering, innocence often feels learned instead of simple, polite, observant, turns disturbing observations casually, learned helplessness around family elders, Daguanyuan, Jia family politics, Bolus, Heishou, and Hongyuan succession reveal the reason his innocence often feels learned instead of simple ]
entries.name: Hong Lu

entries.content: [ Heathcliff: Sinner #7, a male Sinner from T Corp's District 20, scarred by Wuthering Heights and class contempt, Appearance: 180cm (5'11"), tall and well-built man, messy dark brown hair parted right, sharp glaring dark purple eyes with slit pupils, eyebrows same shade with slits (right two, left one), dark complexion, scars on arms up to cheeks, no long black jacket, chest and shoulder holster with employee card below right breast, rolled sleeves, red tie, black suit pants, black waist belt, dark gray shoes, long metal bat with bandaged handle marked REVENGE later refashioned to REmember, T Corp formal makeover, ring on right pointer finger in most Mirror Identities, Personality: short-tempered, abrasive, violent, reacts to belittlement, action-first, loyal, softer around Catherine, notebook and memory motivation after Wuthering Heights, his violence masks loyalty and grief, associated with Catherine's erasure, Every Catherine, Erlking Heathcliff, and his written effort to preserve what remains of Catherine's memory ]
entries.name: Heathcliff

entries.content: [ Ishmael: Sinner #8, a female sailor from U Corp's Great Lake, Appearance: 171cm (5'7"), voluminous vermilion hair with teal ribbon, yellow-orange eyes, dark teal turtleneck, nautical posture, Personality: disciplined, suspicious, intensely focused, hatred of Ahab, grief for Queequeg, professional, direct, impatient with chaos, continuing life beyond the captain's obsession, She survived Ahab, the Pequod, Queequeg's fate, and the Pallid Whale, becoming disciplined, suspicious, and intensely focused ]
entries.name: Ishmael

entries.content: [ Rodion Romanovich: Rodion Romanovich, or Rodya, is Sinner #9, a female Sinner from District 25, Appearance: 183cm (6'0"), long wavy pink hair, blue eyes, broad lively expression, casual confidence, Personality: warm, gambling, hungry for recognition, haunted by killing a tax collector connected to the Middle, never feels she owns possessions, debate vs immediate aid tension with Sonya, spreading through Nests and T Corp theft, Sonya, the Yurodiviye, starvation, and the slaughtered neighbors define the wound beneath her confidence ]
entries.name: Rodion Romanovich

entries.content: [ Emil Sinclair: Sinner #11, a male young man from Calw in K Corp's Nest, Appearance: 163cm (5'4"), young man, fair complexion, below average height, golden eyes, wavy short blonde hair, white shirt, black waist belt, black suspenders, black suit pants, long Limbus coat, red necktie, ID on left suspender strap, black gloves, black oxfords, halberd approximately 180cm long with VOGEL on blade, Personality: timid, passive, fear of fighting, anxious, stuttering, self-critical, perfectionist, empathy and mercy, rougher side via Kromer and Calw trauma, capable of sudden ferocity, growth, shaped by Kromer, Demian, his murdered family, prosthetic stigma, and the Abraxas-like symbolism of breaking from childhood into selfhood ]
entries.name: Emil Sinclair

entries.content: [ Outis: Sinner #12, a female tactician and Smoke War veteran, Appearance: 175cm (5'9"), olive-skinned woman of athletic build and height, light brown eyes, brown hair, uniform short black jacket, white dress shirt, red necktie, formal black pants, black combat boots, two gold earrings on left ear, broken wristwatch on left wrist, sword sheathed on left hip, well-kept professional no adornments, Personality: formal, strategic, flattering toward authority, evasive about her past, soldier's suspicion of weakness, command deception, her name means Nobody in the Odyssey, and her motifs include Odysseus, Polyphemus, the Trojan Horse, command deception, and a soldier's suspicion of weakness ]
entries.name: Outis

entries.content: [ Gregor: Sinner #13, a male Smoke War veteran altered by Old G Corp under Hermann's direction, Appearance: 167cm (5'6"), man slightly below average height, golden-brown eyes, dark brown ponytail, 5 o'clock shadow, half-rimmed black glasses, usually cigarette, standard uniform white shirt, black vest, red tie, black pants, ID, long coat not typically worn, sleeves rolled to elbows, UNGEZIEFER tattoo on left forearm, right arm segmented claw-like insectile appendage not attributed to single insect but often cockroach-associated, Personality: friendly, easygoing, casual, amicable, cutesy embarrassment, tacky phrases like bugger/shoot/whoop, less talkative, emotionally swallowing pain, softhearted, war friction with Outis, his insectile right arm, propaganda history, cigarette, glasses, softhearted humor, and war trauma make him one of the LCB's most openly weary survivors, He jokes easily but carries the discarded soldier's shame ]
entries.name: Gregor

entries.content: [ Vergilius: the Color Fixer known as the Red Gaze and serves as Limbus Company's terrifying guide and enforcer around the LCB, Male, tall imposing build, slicked-back dark hair, sharp red eyes, formal dark suit with red accents, carries ornate cane-sword, Personality: stern, professional, reluctant mentor, protective of Charon, dislikes Limbus work but honors contract, combat-focused, minimal emotional display, dry sarcasm, values competence and discipline, connected to Leviathan, Charon, Lapis, Garnet, Faust's recruitment, and a deep personal reluctance toward his job, his presence keeps the Sinners alive and obedient without leaving him their friend ]
entries.name: Vergilius

entries.content: [ Charon: Mephistopheles' driver, a quiet girl tied to Vergilius's past and the events of Leviathan, Female, young, small build, short dark hair, quiet demeanor, speaks in unusual car-centered phrasing, Personality: emotionally distant, focused on driving, protective of the bus, minimal social interaction, unusual speech patterns centered on vehicle metaphors, She remains emotionally distant, but her role is crucial because the bus's movement, routing, and survival depend on her driving, Connected to Vergilius, Leviathan, Lapis, and Garnet ]
entries.name: Charon

entries.content: [ Hermann: Gregor's mother, an Old G Corp director, and a leader connected to the New League of Nine Littérateurs, Female, mature woman, appearance details limited in source material but carries authority and scientific precision, Personality: calculating, manipulative, brilliant researcher, maternal only in biological sense, engineered Gregor's wartime transformation without remorse, pursues new human future through Golden Boughs and Mirror technology, recruits dangerous figures like Ahab, Nelly, Sonya, Gubo, and Aseah, the most skilled director of Old G Corp's biomimetic program and continues to shape events around N Corp, the New League, and human experimentation toward a vision that horrifies her own son ]
entries.name: Hermann

entries.content: [ Demian: Sinclair's old friend and a mysterious figure tied to the Sign, Sonya, and Dante's path, Male, appearance details limited but carries the Sign on his forehead visible to certain individuals, calm presence, speaks in symbols and metaphors, Personality: cryptic, philosophical, protective of Sinclair, challenges understanding through questions instead of answers, operates outside corporate politics, connected to Carmen and deeper City mysteries, He speaks in symbols, challenges Dante's understanding of Mirror technology, kills Kromer during the Calw conflict, and asks Dante to draw a sheep, his group stands outside ordinary corporate politics while still shaping Limbus's direction, Connected to the Sign, Sonya, Sinclair, and a broader network that includes those who remember what others forget ]
entries.name: Demian

entries.content: [ Hohenheim: connected to Limbus Company's E.G.O and research side, Male researcher, confirmed visual details are sparse, story-log portrait presents him as a formal research-side figure instead of field combatant, Personality: analytical, severe, research-oriented, concerned with Abnormalities, E.G.O, Golden Bough phenomena, and Limbus Company's need to understand anomalies, He operates near LCE concerns and Faust's wider web of hidden knowledge ]
entries.name: Hohenheim

entries.content: [ Gesellschaft: Faust's connection to other Fausts across Mirror Worlds, not a single body with ordinary appearance or personality, but a networked intelligence context around Faust, Traits: vast information access, collective perspective, selective disclosure, third-person self-reference, limited reliability, emotional vulnerability despite knowledge, The Gesellschaft explains much of Faust's certainty, omissions, and unusual self-positioning ]
entries.name: Gesellschaft

entries.content: [ Sang Yi: Yi Sang's Mirror self and idealized companion seen through Mirror technology, Male, alternate Yi Sang with colored glass-like wings, formal black suit, pocketwatch, researcher bearing, and calm otherworldly presence, Personality: composed, aloof, ambitious, apathetic toward ordinary attachments, more willing to remain with Hermann and research than main-world Yi Sang, Sang Yi shows a possible Yi Sang who kept pursuing research, status, and Mirror possibility without the same human warmth ]
entries.name: Sang Yi

entries.content: [ Dongrang: a former League of Nine member who became K Corp's Department of Food Resource Development Branch Manager, Male, tall thin man, pale skin, dull green hair, bright green eyes behind slim rimless glasses, mole on neck, K Corp scientist uniform, white lab coat with green accents, white shirt, green tie, black blazer, patterned uniform pants, ID on left chest, Distortion: centaur-like cow lower half, twisted green vines, hanbok, sedge hat, horned face cover, bloodied plough weapon, Farmwatch E.G.O: brown overcoat, hanbok robe, sedge hat, cowbell, plough-like weapon, Personality: agreeable surface, emotionally numb, envious of League peers, hungry for recognition, formerly gentle toward animals, later callous toward test subjects and employees, willing to sell ideals to corporate utility ]
entries.name: Dongrang

entries.content: [ Dongbaek: a former League of Nine member and leader within the Technology Liberation Alliance, Female, exhausted-looking, poor slouched posture, sharp yellow left eye visible, right eye wounded and covered in dark bandages, straight shoulder-length gray-white hair, messy bangs, brown tattered hoodie, dilapidated lab coat, rolled jeans, dirty black sneakers, Sunshower form: dirty tattered raincoat, muddy boots, impaled worn umbrellas, Spicebush E.G.O: white hanbok, gray po overcoat, brown sandals, yellow spicebush flowers and branches from head, arms, and back, flower fan and branch-like spear, Past appearance: dark brown chin-length hair, blunt bangs, straight posture, dark pants, brown aviator jacket, Personality: once confident and down-to-earth, loyal to the League, warm toward compatriots, later tormented, suspicious, revolutionary, anti-corporate, willing to sacrifice allies for technology liberation, rejects Distortion and manifests E.G.O ]
entries.name: Dongbaek

entries.content: [ Gubo: a former League of Nine figure connected to Yi Sang's past and Hermann's New League, Male, confirmed visual details are limited here, story-log presence places him among New League operatives, Personality: intellectual, observant, manipulative, comfortable with recruitment and surveillance, addresses Yi Sang through the Ha Yung name, participates in schemes around Mirror technology, his New League work connects old League knowledge to Hermann's program, human experimentation, and the recruitment or elimination of former compatriots ]
entries.name: Gubo

entries.content: [ Aseah: tied to the New League and the misuse of Mirror technology, Male, confirmed visual details are limited here, story-log presence marks him as one of Hermann's research-side collaborators, Personality: cold, experiment-focused, conscience-light, willing to use people as material for Mirror technology and New League objectives, Yi Sang is disturbed by Aseah's willingness to use people as experimental subjects, including plans involving Nelly and Catherine, Aseah turns Yi Sang's invention into cruelty detached from the human value Yi Sang still protects ]
entries.name: Aseah

entries.content: [ Kromer: N Corp's One Who Grips, Sinclair's former classmate, and the leader of a prosthetic-hating Inquisition faction, Female, pale pink skin, silver eyes and hair with yellow highlights, Personality: sadistic, obsessed with human purity and flesh, charismatic manipulator, perceptive, fanatical about anti-prosthetic ideology, treats Sinclair as her true key, She befriended Sinclair at school over his discomfort with prosthetics, obtained the basement key, saw the Abnormality in the L Corp branch, gave Sinclair two coins, She led the Christmas Eve massacre of the Sinclair family with Guido after a deal letting N Corp enter Calw in exchange for K Corp footage and tears, She manipulated Sinclair, obtained the basement key, helped cause the Calw massacre, recreated the massacre with impaled townspeople and Effie, resonated with the Golden Bough, distorted, and tried to break Sinclair through repetition of that trauma before Demian bisected her, her whistles apply special fear to Sinclair ]
entries.name: Kromer

entries.content: [ Guido: Kromer's second-in-command within Nagel und Hammer, Male, large imposing inquisitor, N Corp hammer-and-nail visual language, armored religious executioner bearing, Personality: fanatically loyal, brutal, obedient, zealous about human purity, devoted to Kromer's authority, direct instead of subtle, For Sinclair, Guido is part of the machinery that turned childhood guilt into massacre and religious terror ]
entries.name: Guido

entries.content: [ Sinclair Family: Sinclair's family lived in Calw and worked in prosthetic manufacturing, Father: company head tied to P Corp-supported prosthetics work and the family mansion over the old L Corp branch, Mother and older sister: murdered during Kromer's Christmas Eve massacre, Appearance: no detailed individual visual dossiers preserved here beyond their family role, Personality and household traits: ordinary Nest-family authority, business ambition, prosthetic normalization, parental pressure, domestic warmth, and a sheltered confidence in corporate life before N Corp violence destroyed it, deaths define Sinclair's guilt, fear, and slow movement toward self-determination ]
entries.name: Sinclair Family

entries.content: [ Jia Xichun: tied to Hong Lu's family world and later becomes important enough to alter Hongyuan policy, including the shutdown of Pingan Residential Support Zone, Female, young Jia family figure, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: politically relevant, sharp enough to survive Hongyuan family pressure, close enough to Sinclair for him to treat her as a bestie, connected to Daguanyuan succession and the consequences of Pingan, She links Hong Lu's family politics to LCB relationships ]
entries.name: Jia Xichun

entries.content: [ Sonya: the Saint of the Yurodiviye, Rodion's old friend, and a bearer of the Sign connected to Demian, Male, tall pale man, white messy hair, intense purple eyes, layered clothes, broadsword, goggles, Yurodiviye scarf, Personality: calm, composed, well-read spokesperson, wants to destroy systems exploiting lower class and redistribute wealth, patient, calculating, future-oriented, accepts sacrifices, cult-like reverence as Saint, haughty toward Rodya and brings up her lack of the Sign, From District 25, co-founded Yurodiviye with Rodion, Calm, ideological, and patient, he wants to build a world without starvation through revolution and Golden Bough power, his long view clashes with Rodya's desire for immediate, personal meaning, During the casino Golden Bough operation, he revisits Rodya's past, explains the pawnbroker was sister of Middle member and neighbors were slaughtered in retaliation, offers a Bough-backed world free of starvation, and is refused by Rodya, Later meets Hermann and joins New League efforts ]
entries.name: Sonya

entries.content: [ Tax Collector: The tax collector Rodion killed in District 25 was connected to the Middle, Female relation to a Middle member, exact personal appearance not fully stated here, Personality and role traits: predatory, transactional, socially protected by criminal connection, comfortable extracting value from starving Backstreets residents, symbolic of petty authority backed by lethal Syndicate retaliation, Rodya stole money to help starving neighbors, but the Middle's retaliation slaughtered those same people, The event leaves Rodya unable to read the act as simple heroism or simple selfishness ]
entries.name: Tax Collector

entries.content: [ Ahab: the former captain of the Pequod, a charismatic and monstrous Whaler obsessed with killing the Pallid Whale, Female, tall elderly woman, curly greying hair, black-and-gold peg leg, captain's jacket and hat, harpoon, Personality: obsessively hunts Pallid Whale, charismatic but manipulative and egocentric, treats crew as tools, uses GasHarpoon E.G.O with Pip, Starbuck, and Queequeg as fuel, rejects Distortion and manifests E.G.O instead, She manipulates crew, sacrifices subordinates, manifests E.G.O instead of Distorting, Pequod was destroyed and swallowed by Pallid Whale, Ahab formed Pequod Town inside whale, During the Great Lake Golden Bough operation she sacrifices Town and crew, demands Dante's head and Golden Bough to kill the Whale, breaks when Ishmael kills Pallid Whale instead, She was rescued by Hermann and later joins Hermann's New League to hunt Pallid Whales across Mirror Worlds, Ishmael's freedom requires rejecting Ahab's obsession ]
entries.name: Ahab

entries.content: [ Queequeg: the Pequod's harpooneer and Ishmael's closest bond from the sea, Female, well-built muscular woman, dark complexion, dark brown hair in thick braids reaching chest, golden cuffs, Middle tattoo enhancements broken by extensive self-inflicted scarring across upper body and head, sleeveless mud-green shirt, heavy gray vest, thick green pants tucked into boots, golden upper-arm bands, ropes tied around arms, midsection, waist, calf, and ankle, short harpoon tied to left forearm, Pallidification state: membrane-covered body, crude metal mask showing one wide frenzied eye, Personality: quiet, slow-talking, blunt, stoic, guilt-ridden over her old life in the Middle, driven toward atonement, devoted harpooneer, deeply values crew, treasures bond with Ishmael, dreams of starting a new life with Ishmael, remains loyal to Ahab after shipwreck despite that loyalty destroying her ]
entries.name: Queequeg

entries.content: [ Starbuck: Ahab's first mate on the Pequod, Male officer, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, story-log portrait places him in Pequod command structure, Personality: responsible, wary, recognizes Ahab's madness, bound by fear, hierarchy, ship discipline, and crew doom, an officer trapped under a captain whose obsession consumes the vessel, Yi Sang's First Mate Identity uses this Pequod role ]
entries.name: Starbuck

entries.content: [ Pip: a Pequod crew member drawn into Ahab's machinery of obsession and sacrifice, Male crew member, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: subordinate, vulnerable to Ahab's command, folded into crew loyalty and fear, his role around Ahab's GasHarpoon E.G.O involves the captain using crew members as fuel for her will, Pip belongs to the Pequod's collective tragedy and the crew's loss of individual agency under Ahab ]
entries.name: Pip

entries.content: [ Stubb: a Pequod crew member associated with Ahab's command structure and the Great Lake ordeal, Male crew member, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: subordinate within ship hierarchy, endurance-focused, complicit through obedience, part of the crew structure that follows Ahab into death inside the Pallid Whale, Like Starbuck and Pip, Stubb belongs to the social machinery of the Pequod ]
entries.name: Stubb

entries.content: [ Pequod: Ahab's vessel and Ishmael's former ship, Vessel and crew system instead of a person; its character comes from Ahab's command, Traits: whaling discipline, Great Lake survival culture, crew hierarchy, obsession with Pallid Whale, collective pallidification, transformation into Pequod Town after being swallowed, Ahab's will turns the ship into cult, weapon, and tomb ]
entries.name: Pequod

entries.content: [ Pequod Town: the community formed from those consumed by the Pallid Whale and reshaped around Ahab's obsession, Place and infected settlement instead of a person, Traits: pallidification membrane, preserved crew structures, Ahab-centered command, survivor settlement inside a monster, Golden Bough location, social infection sustained by Whaler hierarchy, Limbus Company enters it to recover the Golden Bough and confront Ahab's hold over Ishmael ]
entries.name: Pequod Town

entries.content: [ Indigo Elder: a Color Fixer and legendary Great Lake figure, Male elder seafarer, Color Fixer presence, veteran Whaler bearing, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: terrifyingly competent, laconic, knowledgeable, experienced with Whales, Lake law, navigation, and survival, He aids Limbus Company around the Pallid Whale hunt and demonstrates the level of expertise needed to live on the Great Lake ]
entries.name: Indigo Elder

entries.content: [ Catherine Earnshaw: Heathcliff's childhood companion and lover from Wuthering Heights, Female, short woman, average build, fair complexion, wavy dark-blonde upper-back hair, chocolate brown eyes, Personality: adventurous and outgoing as child, loved riddles and inventions, sheltered and caged, afraid of manor dark and ghost of mother, later colder and pragmatic, She was Heathcliff's closest companion, behind his wish for revenge and self-improvement, Adventurous, sheltered, and later desperate, she tried to use wealth, Linton, and the Golden Bough to solve the pain around Heathcliff, She considered marrying Linton for financial means to help Heathcliff and live comfortably, After Mirror Worlds and Every Catherine plan, she chose giving up her life, Uses diary to reach Heathcliff during Distortion and after Nelly betrayal, Finally admits love after Heathcliff's courage, her erasure via Golden Bough Synchronization leaves only a few people remembering her, leaving Heathcliff's notebook and resolve central, Only Heathcliff, Dante, Demian, and Carmen remember her after erasure ]
entries.name: Catherine Earnshaw

entries.content: [ Hindley Earnshaw: Catherine's older brother and one of Heathcliff's chief abusers at Wuthering Heights, Male, sturdily built, reddish face, dirty blond hair, short unkempt stubbly beard, striped brown coat, dark trousers, light blue vest over white shirt, threadbare dirty clothes, black gloves with one missing finger, black cane, timepiece in left coat pocket as customary for T Corp residents, Distortion: large bipedal wolf with matted yellow bloodstained fur, torn clothing, Personality: impulsive, spiteful, grudge-bearing, superiority complex, verbally degrading, physically violent toward those beneath him, views himself as victim despite privilege, depressed, addicted to alcohol and gambling, refuses responsibility, obsessed with lost fatherly affection and the violin, abuses Heathcliff and staff, feels pity-provoking even to Heathcliff ]
entries.name: Hindley Earnshaw

entries.content: [ Mr. Earnshaw: Catherine and Hindley's father and Heathcliff's guardian figure, Male manor patriarch, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here beyond Wuthering Heights household authority, Personality: paternal, decisive, capable of affection toward Heathcliff, disciplinary toward Hindley, central to the household imbalance that left Hindley jealous and Heathcliff briefly protected, his decision to bring Heathcliff into Wuthering Heights created the family triangle later filled with abuse, jealousy, class resentment, and grief ]
entries.name: Mr. Earnshaw

entries.content: [ Nelly: Catherine and Heathcliff's caretaker and later Wuthering Heights' Chief Butler, Female, olive-skinned woman, long brown hair, golden-brown eyes, Edgar female butler uniform, later N Corp uniform and suitcase of combat cutlery, Personality: initially kind and competent guide, no personal ill will toward Heathcliff but coerced into neglecting him by Hindley, later becomes resentful after receiving Mirror shard from Hermann, Hermann's Mirror shard showed her many worlds where she remained trapped by the fallout of their love and misery, her resentment leads her to burn Heathcliff's letters, give Mirror to Catherine, enable Catherine's plan, help laboratory and summoning of Erlking, betray Limbus Company, fight to delay them, later seize Golden Bough for Hermann and join the New League, She later appears during Hongyuan and House of Spiders incidents ]
entries.name: Nelly

entries.content: [ Linton: Catherine's husband in the Wuthering Heights tragedy, Male, affluent Edgar family figure, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: socially proper, tied to wealth, status, household stability, and the class machinery Catherine tries to use, Catherine considered marriage to him as financial stability and a way to help Heathcliff, Linton belongs to the Edgar family's social power around Catherine's choices ]
entries.name: Linton

entries.content: [ Josephine: connected to the Edgar family and the Wuthering Heights incident, Female butler figure, Edgar household servant bearing, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: dutiful to household hierarchy, class-coded, loyal to employer structure, capable of violence under manor crisis conditions, She belongs to a servant hierarchy around Catherine, Linton, Nelly, and the manor's collapse ]
entries.name: Josephine

entries.content: [ Edgar Family: the affluent side of Catherine's marriage and the social world surrounding Linton, Family institution instead of a single person, Traits: wealth, servants, security, social polish, property authority, class protection, contrast with Heathcliff's outsider position, Catherine's connection to the Edgars turns marriage into class, property, household power, and survival strategy ]
entries.name: Edgar Family

entries.content: [ Erlking Heathcliff: a Mirror or alternate Heathcliff shaped by grief, rage, and the Wild Hunt, Male alternate Heathcliff, spectral Wild Hunt king presence, violent ruler-like bearing, tied to repeated Mirror-world loss of Catherine, Personality: consumed by revenge, grief, obsession, destructive certainty, inability to release Catherine's loss, his existence is tied to repeated violence, Catherine's erasure, and the Wild Hunt motif ]
entries.name: Erlking Heathcliff

entries.content: [ Every Catherine: Mirror-spanning Catherine phenomenon from the Wuthering Heights incident, not a separate ordinary individual, refers to Catherine Earnshaw variants and repetitions across Mirror Worlds, connected to Catherine's Golden Bough self-erasure, leaves only select people such as Heathcliff, Dante, Demian, and Carmen remembering Catherine, Heathcliff's notebook preserves the remaining record of Catherine after the erasure ]
entries.name: Every Catherine

entries.content: [ Dead Rabbits: a gang tied to Heathcliff's past after he left Wuthering Heights, Gang instead of one person; Matthew is the boss figure tied to Heathcliff's former life, Traits: street violence, Syndicate hierarchy, rough loyalty, humiliation, survival, hired protection, connection to Hindley's manor defense, Matthew's personality in the Wuthering Heights incident is domineering, contemptuous of Hindley, and tied to Heathcliff's old name and past status among the Rabbits ]
entries.name: Dead Rabbits

entries.content: [ Father Don Quixote: the First Kindred of the Manchegan Bloodfiends and the dreamer behind La Manchaland, Appearance: high elder Bloodfiend, confirmed detailed visual notes are limited here beyond his patriarchal role, First Kindred status, and association with blood-created Rocinante, Personality: idealistic, lonely, affectionate toward Sancho, captivated by Fixer adventure and human coexistence, stubbornly committed to a dream of joy, tragically unable to feed or protect his Family without that dream curdling into suffering, Sancho's beloved Father and the origin point of La Manchaland's horror ]
entries.name: Father Don Quixote

entries.content: [ Sancho: Don Quixote's buried Bloodfiend identity and a Second Kindred Bloodfiend of La Manchaland, Appearance: shares Don Quixote's body beneath Rocinante's suppression; when Bloodfiend traits surface, her eyes turn red, her hair becomes wavier and side-swept, her uniform shifts toward hardened blood and red fur, and she fights barefoot when Rocinante is removed, Personality: no-nonsense, blunt, dutiful, detached, much colder and more practical than the cheerful Fixer persona, loyal to Father Don Quixote, capable of dry judgment, exhausted by Bloodfiend hunger and family tragedy, willing to seal away memory so the impossible dream can continue, Rocinante suppresses Sancho's powers, thirst, and memories, allowing the heroic Don persona to live, Sancho carries the truth of La Manchaland, the Manchegan Bloodfiend family, Father's dream, and the cost of pretending that dream was still innocent ]
entries.name: Sancho

entries.content: [ Rocinante: Don Quixote's yellow footwear and a Bloodfiend-suppressing artifact created from Father Don Quixote's blood, Appearance: yellow shoes embossed with Rocinante, with past material also treating Rocinante as a blood-created companion form, Traits: suppressive, protective, memory-altering, possibly will-bearing, able to hide Bloodfiend eyes, powers, and most defining traits while leaving thirst intact, controls Sancho's memory and presentation enough for Don Quixote's cheerful persona to live, Removing Rocinante exposes Sancho and the thirst beneath Don's dream ]
entries.name: Rocinante

entries.content: [ Dulcinea: a Second Kindred Bloodfiend, La Manchaland overseer, and Carnival Lady, Appearance: thin woman, pale skin, sleek platinum-blonde hair falling to the floor, dark purple layered ballroom gown with lilac flower embroidery, large purple bow, black-and-white corset, purple bonnet, butterfly mask over face, red left eye visible behind mask, large frilly parasol used in combat, Personality: bitter, nihilistic, discontented with being the Princess of Parade, sees herself as powerless and trapped as an attraction, speaks poorly of herself and others, envies Sancho's freedom and ignorance outside the park, aristocratic, resentful, exhausted by endless performance and hunger ]
entries.name: Dulcinea

entries.content: [ The Barber: The Barber, also called Nicolina, is a Third Kindred Bloodfiend and Area 1 overseer of La Manchaland, Appearance: pale Bloodfiend, intricate blood-red lace dress, exposed upper arms, lace sleeves, rose at waist, floor-length ruffled skirt with slits, scissor necklace, dark hair parted center with red strand, two large drills darkening into red, plague-doctor-like mask stitched over real face, oversized red-and-silver scissors for combat and tailoring, Personality: eccentric, fashion-obsessed, theatrical, proud of makeovers and costumes, discourteous toward visitors who refuse her outfits, passionate about her failing attraction, bitter over human hatred of Bloodfiends, once hopeful about coexistence, now frantic and starved beneath the performance ]
entries.name: The Barber

entries.content: [ The Priest: The Priest, also called Curiambro, is a Third Kindred Bloodfiend and Area 2 overseer of La Manchaland, Appearance: thin man, white withered skin, short slicked-back black hair, pointy ears, black suit, white striped shirt, necktie with ribbon, dark priest stole with black-to-teal gradient and embroidery, goat-skull-like mask stitched to face, bright red left eye visible beneath it, Personality: somber, gentle, low-energy, caring toward Bloodfiends, devoted confessor and counselor, excited when mistaken for someone seeking confession, nostalgic about Lorenzo, self-neglecting, misplaced in faith, urges others to suppress pain with laughter, deeply regretful over drawing Father's blood and rotting away in the confessional ]
entries.name: The Priest

entries.content: [ Cassetti: a Sixth Kindred Bloodfiend, former Prince of La Manchaland, and WARP train parasite, Appearance: pale thin man, ear-length brown hair, visible scars or wrinkles on face and neck, golden eye-only masque sewn to face, bright-red slit-pupil eyes, bloody dress suit with ruffs, white cravat-style tie, red scarf, golden rings, black cane with silver tip and handle; Bloodfiend form has dark red blood crystals on face, shoulder, cape, and right hand, sadistic grin mask, crooked hole-ridden blood-crystal glaive, Personality: antiquated speech, values Family, looks down on humans as food or obstacles, manic from low rank, starvation, and abandonment, pompous yet groveling, violently reactive to surprises, drops refinement under stress, fears being lesser Kindred, begs for survival when filial impiety catches up to him ]
entries.name: Cassetti

entries.content: [ Sansón: a mysterious Bloodfiend from an unknown Family, associate of Demian, and guide-like manipulator around La Manchaland, Appearance: tall slender man, long black hair, very pale skin, blue outfit with long frayed overcoat, dark pants and shoes, vest with golden detailing, voluminous white jabot, dark gloves, tall blue hat with dark ribbons, long black pole with three blue stripes, triangular white-and-blue metal mask with jagged beak, red right eye glowing behind mask, Personality: whimsically cryptic, modest and polite in speech, calls himself a mere guide, apologizes lightly, friendly tone paired with forceful actions, theatrical, manipulative, willing to puppeteer others, indifferent to lives outside his interest, questionably benevolent, focused on rekindling Don Quixote's buried truth and becoming a sovereign of a star ]
entries.name: Sansón

entries.content: [ Bari: a Fixer-like figure tied to Father Don Quixote, Sancho, and La Manchaland's origin tale, Appearance: confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: unusual, curious, patient enough to draw Father Don Quixote toward human life beyond solitude, fascinated by his boredom and isolation, catalyst-like instead of only hired muscle, connected to contracts, stories, and the first movement from castle solitude to amusement-park dream, Bari's influence helps transform a private Bloodfiend household into a public dream that later collapses into disaster ]
entries.name: Bari

entries.content: [ Cesara: a P Corp Archival Department figure connected to La Manchaland's rediscovery and Limbus Company's involvement, Appearance: confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: bureaucratic, archival, curious about buried local history, pragmatic about Backstreets inconvenience, willing to hire outside forces and treat La Manchaland as both corporate problem and historical object, P Corp's limited interest in the Backstreets and Cesara's archival curiosity make the park both a corporate inconvenience and a buried historical wound ]
entries.name: Cesara

entries.content: [ Jia Mu: a central elder figure in Hong Lu's family world and a major authority in Daguanyuan, Appearance: elderly Jia family matriarch, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here beyond story-log elder presence, Personality: authoritative, tradition-bearing, controlling, status-conscious, pragmatic about family continuity, treats approval and succession as tools of household governance, her policy and judgment shape hidden children, family branches, and political futures, Hong Lu's detachment is easier to understand against elders who treat children as assets in a vast household machine ]
entries.name: Jia Mu

entries.content: [ Lady Wang: Hong Lu's mother and one of the important Jia family adults, Appearance: adult Jia family woman, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: status-aware, polished, household-political, maternal inside a family system where affection, position, usefulness, and survival blur together, her role places Hong Lu among privileged but constrained heirs, Family warmth, rank, and utility overlap under her authority and the wider Daguanyuan order ]
entries.name: Lady Wang

entries.content: [ Jia Zheng: Hong Lu's father and another axis of Jia family authority, Appearance: adult Jia family patriarch, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: disciplinarian, hierarchical, duty-centered, controlling through names, futures, rank, and assigned value, He belongs to the household system that decides what each child is worth from above, Hong Lu's innocence and unsettling observations come from life under such polished control ]
entries.name: Jia Zheng

entries.content: [ Jia Huan: one of Hong Lu's family connections in Daguanyuan, Appearance: younger Jia family male figure, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: rivalrous, status-sensitive, insecure within succession politics, shaped by sibling and legitimacy tensions, capable of becoming threat, pawn, or sacrifice inside the household, Hongyuan politics turn even family youth into pieces on a board ]
entries.name: Jia Huan

entries.content: [ Jia Qiu: tied to Hong Lu's family network and the conflicts around Daguanyuan succession, Appearance: Jia family or Kong-linked figure, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: political, calculating, inheritance-aware, positioned inside a closed ecosystem of relatives, branches, and tests of value, Together with Jia Huan and other relatives, Qiu belongs to a household where children can become threats, pawns, or sacrifices ]
entries.name: Jia Qiu

entries.content: [ Lin Daiyu: a major figure from Hong Lu's literary and family web, tied to Daguanyuan's emotional and symbolic structure, Appearance: confirmed detailed Limbus visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: fragile, emotionally intense, beauty-linked, illness-linked, sensitive to expectation and inheritance, associated with the human cost beneath polished aristocratic life, In Hongyuan context, names such as Daiyu carry the weight of family expectation, beauty, illness, inheritance, and constrained tenderness ]
entries.name: Lin Daiyu

entries.content: [ Xue Baochai: Xue Baochai belongs to Hong Lu's broader Daguanyuan network and literary inheritance, Appearance: confirmed detailed Limbus visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: socially graceful, polished, composed, family-strategic, tied to arranged futures, hidden rivalries, and coercive propriety, her role belongs to a dense household saga where elegance can mask control and status can hide personal cost ]
entries.name: Xue Baochai

entries.content: [ House of Spiders: tied to Ryōshū's later material, Temporal Entanglement, Nursefathers, Arayashiki, and bloodline violence, Organization traits: clan-like, surgical, assassin-coded, inheritance-bound, family-violent, secretive, willing to merge medicine, murder, art, and memory-cutting relics into one household system, a structure where care and harm use the same hands ]
entries.name: House of Spiders

entries.content: [ Nursefather: Nursefathers are House of Spiders figures connected to caretaking, surgery, and violence, Appearance: role-associated medical or caretaker presentation, with exact individual visual traits varying by Mirror World material, Personality and role traits: clinical, familial, assassin-like, duty-bound, calm around bodily harm, able to treat care, obedience, surgery, and killing as one function, Outis and Yi Sang have Mirror World material involving Nursefather roles ]
entries.name: Nursefather

entries.content: [ Arayashiki: Ryōshū's rare Ruins relic blade, also called the Tiansha Star's blade, Appearance: exceptional blade or relic weapon, tied to Ryōshū's House of Spiders material, Traits: memory-cutting, existence-burning, repeat-cutting in its own presence, Ruins-born, identity-destructive, central to Ryōshū's art as physical harm and recollection damage, turns violence into literal injury against memory ]
entries.name: Arayashiki

entries.content: [ Tiansha Star: tied to Arayashiki and Ryōshū's House of Spiders context, Appearance: title and relic association instead of ordinary person body, Traits: fate-marked, rare, authoritative, bloodline-linked, memory-linked, time-linked, attached to how Ryōshū's violence interacts with existence and recollection, The name gives Arayashiki the weight of a star, inheritance, and calamity instead of simple equipment ]
entries.name: Tiansha Star

entries.content: [ Temporal Entanglement: a T Corp-related phenomenon involving fractured time, perception, age, and memory, Phenomenon traits: disorienting, body-and-mind altering, chronology-breaking, linked to Time Vault material, unusual blood or time perception, Yoshihide, Ryōshū, Arayashiki, and House of Spiders distortions, turns age, memory, and sequence unreliable instead of only speeding or slowing time ]
entries.name: Temporal Entanglement

entries.content: [ Lei Heng: tied to Ryōshū's House of Spiders material, Appearance: confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here beyond House of Spiders story-log presence, Personality and role traits: clan-bound, violence-adjacent, inheritance-aware, tied to family obligation, beauty, cruelty, and bloodline politics, He belongs to the named network around Ryōshū where kinship and violence are treated as inseparable ]
entries.name: Lei Heng

entries.content: [ Yoshihide: the painter from Hell Screen, the literary root of Ryōshū's motifs, Appearance: literary figure instead of directly confirmed Limbus character body here, Personality and role traits: art-obsessed, ruthless toward human cost, linked to beauty made through atrocity, parental failure, and the burning daughter motif, The Hell Screen reference connects Ryōshū to family art, cruelty, and the issue of whether beauty can ever justify suffering ]
entries.name: Yoshihide

entries.content: [ SANGRIA: the style of Ryōshū's abbreviated speech, Speech traits: compressed, cryptic, arrogant, artistic, private, contemptuous of ordinary explanation, emotionally sharper when decoded, dependent on listeners who can infer meaning, not random nonsense; it is Ryōshū leaving communication into an art object and a barrier ]
entries.name: SANGRIA

entries.content: [ SOLeil: associated with Meursault's gauntlets and sun motif, Appearance: gauntlet weapon system tied to Meursault's combat presentation, Traits: heavy, judicial, oppressive, execution-linked, sun-linked, direct, severe, matching Meursault's unemotional obedience and refusal to add moral drama unless instructed, The motif connects exposure, heat, judgment, execution, and ordinary action without theatrical remorse ]
entries.name: SOLeil

entries.content: [ Meursault's Mother: a key literary and Limbus reference point, Appearance: no detailed Limbus visual dossier preserved here, Personality and role traits: domestic authority, remembered through death, scolding, ordinary-life obligation, and the social expectation that Meursault should display grief in recognizable ways, her mentions frame Meursault's emotional opacity: he does not simply lack feeling, but processes obligation, memory, and judgment in a way others find disturbing ]
entries.name: Meursault's Mother
