entries.content: [ Odyssey: Outis draws on the Odyssey, especially Odysseus calling himself Nobody to deceive Polyphemus, Motif traits: hidden identity, long return, military cunning, deceptive names, endurance, leadership, pride, survival through lies and timing, The name Outis, written ΟΥΤΙΣ, signals a commander who uses identity as a tool while Limbus keeps her actual past hidden ]
entries.name: Odyssey

entries.content: [ Trojan Horse: The Trojan Horse motif is part of Outis's strategy language, Motif traits: victory through deception, patient infiltration, concealed violence, gift-as-weapon logic, command discipline, willingness to hide intent until decisive action, connects to her military past, command instincts, flattery of Dante, and concealed personal history ]
entries.name: Trojan Horse

entries.content: [ Yuri: a former L Corp employee encountered early in Limbus Company's journey, Appearance: young woman, story-log support personnel presence, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality: vulnerable, earnest, frightened but cooperative, connected to old corporate trauma, more ordinary and fragile than the Sinners' contract-protected recklessness, her death in the branch expedition gives Gregor and the LCB a painful lesson in how old corporate victims are still being consumed by current missions ]
entries.name: Yuri

entries.content: [ Aya: part of the early Limbus branch-team context with Yuri and Hopkins, Appearance: ordinary Limbus support personnel, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: professional, field-support oriented, mortal in a way the Sinners are not, bound to dangerous preparatory work without Dante's revival contract, her role marks the ordinary employees whose deaths remain permanent around LCB operations ]
entries.name: Aya

entries.content: [ Hopkins: tied to the early branch-team incident involving Yuri and Gregor, Appearance: ordinary branch-team support figure, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: self-preserving, morally compromised, pragmatic under pressure, willing to expose the ugliness of survival calculations around Limbus fieldwork, his behavior contrasts with Yuri's vulnerability and the Sinners' contract-protected recklessness ]
entries.name: Hopkins

entries.content: [ Tomah: connected to Old G Corp propaganda and Gregor's wartime image, Appearance: old G Corp propaganda-side figure, confirmed detailed visual traits are limited here, Personality and role traits: managerial, image-conscious, exploitative, comfortable turning child soldiers, biomimetic research, and staged heroism into public messaging, He belongs to the machinery that made Gregor's body both weapon and advertisement ]
entries.name: Tomah

entries.content: [ Roach Emperor: the title or layer associated with Gregor in Old G Corp's pest-weapon program, Weapon-program traits: royal insect title, biomimetic hierarchy, propaganda-friendly naming, body-horror augmentation, disposable soldier logic, Hermann's hidden deeper design, The name places Gregor's insect arm inside a larger system of engineered war symbols instead of a single accident ]
entries.name: Roach Emperor

entries.content: [ Maggot Prince: one of Old G Corp's named pest-weapon concepts, Weapon-program traits: royal title, larval decay imagery, grotesque biomimetic body design, propaganda masking, disposable soldier use, sibling concept to Moth Princess, Lord of Flies, and Roach Emperor, belongs to the corporation's mythology for insectoid soldiers whose bodies were treated as tools ]
entries.name: Maggot Prince

entries.content: [ Moth Princess: another Old G Corp pest-weapon concept, Weapon-program traits: fairytale title, insectoid elegance masking surgery, propaganda beautification, expendable soldier body, forced transformation, contrast between princess language and battlefield monstrosity, belongs beside Roach Emperor, Maggot Prince, and Lord of Flies as part of Old G Corp's aestheticized biomimetic war system ]
entries.name: Moth Princess

entries.content: [ Lord of Flies: part of Old G Corp's named insectoid weapon mythology, Weapon-program traits: swarm imagery, decay, command-title grandeur, biomimetic warfare, propaganda language, mass disposability, horror hidden behind noble or mythic naming, sits beside Roach Emperor, Maggot Prince, and Moth Princess as evidence that Gregor's transformation belonged to an organized system of war-symbols ]
entries.name: Lord of Flies

entries.content: [ Gregor's Sister: Gregor has a younger sister connected to the ordinary family life that Old G Corp and Hermann damaged, Appearance: no detailed visual dossier preserved here, Personality and role traits: ordinary family presence, reminder of Gregor's life outside propaganda and experimentation, emotional anchor for the person he was before war converted him into a weapon, References to her sharpen the tragedy of Gregor's past: he is not only a soldier or failed experiment, but someone whose body and reputation were taken from a family context ]
entries.name: Gregor's Sister
